key_insight: Layer-by-layer electrosprayed coatings combining niosomes with ultrasound- and microwave-modified casein/gum arabic complexes can substantially improve Bacillus coagulans viability and protection.
engineering_relevance: The study demonstrates a low-temperature encapsulation strategy that could translate to more stable probiotic delivery systems for food and pharmaceutical manufacturing.
method: Ultrasound pre-treated casein/gum arabic complexes followed by microwave-assisted glycation at 150, 350, and 550 W
Formed probiotic nanoniosomes using Span 20 and Tween 80 and loaded them into electrospraying solutions
Electrosprayed multilayer coatings onto B. coagulans at ambient temperature
evidence_signal: The 350 W complex showed the highest glycation and denaturation temperature, and 15% casein/gum arabic with Span 20 nanoniosomes achieved the best viability at 97.85 ± 1.64%.
watch_item: Performance appears formulation- and process-sensitive, so scale-up must control electrospraying stability, microwave power, and coating uniformity.
paper_title: Layer-by-layer coating of electrosprayed Bacillus coagulans using niosomes and casein/gum arabic complexes induced by ultrasound and microwave-assisted interactions
paper_category: AI materials, welding, and machine design
